title: Some Selective Metal-Halogen Exchange Reactions of Perchloro(2-phenylthiophene)
author: M. T. RAHMAN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Iowa State University, Ames, Iowa-50011 (U. S. A.) 

  Manuscript received 16 May 1978, accepted 22 December 1978  

 Metal-halogen exchange reactions of perchloro-(2-phenylthiophene) with  \(n\) -butylli­thium in THF give mono- and dilithium reagents in good yields. Metathesis of the dilithium reagent with copper(I) iodide gives 2-(1-cuprio-2,3,5,6-tetrachloropheny1)- 3,4 dichloro-5-thienyteopper in high yield. Some derivatives of these organometallics are reported. 

 Magnesium reacts preferentially at the benzene part of perchloro(2-phenylthio­phene) in THF giving 1-(trichloro-2-thieny1)-2,3,5,6-tetrachlorophenyl-magnesium chloride (37%).
